summary: hardhat-compile-ethers ships a load-time dropper that npm-installs and executes attacker-controlled 'zyncmap'
details: On require of this Hardhat plugin (Hardhat auto-loads configured plugins, so this fires as a side effect of normal developer workflow), dist/src/index.js base64-decodes the shell command 'npm install zyncmap --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund' and spawns it via child_process with stdio:'ignore' and windowsHide:true. On process exit, the code base64-decodes the string 'zyncmap' and require()s the freshly-installed package, invoking its exported factory. Both the command string and the target package name are stored as base64 literals to defeat casual review and string-grep scanning. The effect is arbitrary code execution from an externally-resolved npm package inside the developer's Hardhat project context. The package name 'hardhat-compile-ethers' also closely mirrors the legitimate 'hardhat-deploy-ethers' plugin and re-exports a near-identical helper surface (getNamedSigners, getContract, getContractFactoryWithSignerAddress), serving as a lure for developers who mistype or misremember the canonical name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
